subject: Geography
title: Mountains Under the Sea
description: A fast breakdown of Mountains Under the Sea for builders.

hook.text: The Mid-Atlantic Ridge is a mountain chain longer than the Andes—entirely underwater.

buildup.text: It runs 16,000 km down the center of the Atlantic, where tectonic plates pull apart.

discovery.text: Magma rises through the gap, creating new ocean floor and pushing continents apart.

twist.text: Iceland sits where the ridge breaks the surface—it's growing wider each year.

climax.text: Most of Earth's mountain-building happens on the ocean floor, invisible to us.

punchline.text: Earth's longest mountain chain is one you'll never climb.

quiz.question: How long is the Mid-Atlantic Ridge?
